chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-98 of 2020 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For hearing of M.A.3805/2021 For hearing of M.A. 3806/2021 07.12.2021 Petitioner present in person. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Addl. A.G Sindh along with Mr. Aijaz Ahmed Shaikh SSP Dadu, Abdul Khaliq Wagan DSP Dadu, Pannah Ali Shah PDSP Dadu and SIP Noor Muhammad on behalf of DIG Hyderabad. -.-.-. After hearing the parties, we have come to realize that main grievance of the petitioner is about arrest of absconders in his case bearing Crime No.319/2009 of P.S Dadu which is regarding his kidnapping for ransom despite directions of this court. SSP Dadu and DSP Dadu are present and submit that they have made all efforts but meanwhile case has been challaned and four accused have been arrested in the case. We, therefore, of the view that now it is for the trial court to initiate proceedings including 87 & 88 CrPC for arrest of the absconders and has powers to declare absconders as proclaimed offenders issue order permanent in nature to the police for their arrest. Therefore, petitioner may file a proper application before the trial court for arrest of the absconders and whenever such application is filed by the petitioner, the trial court is directed to attend to his application and direct the police concerned for making efforts for the arrest of the absconders and initiate proceedings against them. We have been further informed that in the trial the evidence of material witnesses has been recorded. In the circumstances, the trial court shall expedite the trial and conclude it within a reasonable period preferably not more than three months from passing of this order.

We have been further informed that in the trial the evidence of material witnesses has been recorded. In the circumstances, the trial court shall expedite the trial and conclude it within a reasonable period preferably not more than three months from passing of this order. Listed applications stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE JUDGE Ali Haider
